prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
## Done

The last period lands like a stone
dropped into still water—
finally, finally,
the ripples going out.

My hands don't know what to do now,
hover over the desk like lost birds.
For months I fed this thing,
worried its edges, carried it to bed.

And now the folder closes.
The room is the same room,
the coffee cooling in the same cup,
but something has shifted—

the weight I'd forgotten I was holding
set down so gently
I almost didn't notice
I was free.
